evals.prompt: 请为 design tree 系统补一条共享规则：当原树开始稳定回答第二个核心问题，并且这个问题反复出现、有自己的输入输出和完成标准时，应考虑派生第二棵树。
evals.expected_output: The skill should accept this as valid core content because it is a stable shared derivation rule affecting multiple design skills. Output should classify it as derivation or shared governance rather than rejecting it as project-specific detail.

assertions.name: accepts_shared_derivation_rule
assertions.description: Output accepts the proposed addition as valid shared core content rather than rejecting it

assertions.name: classifies_as_shared_rule
assertions.description: Output identifies that the rule belongs to derivation, boundaries, or anti-bloat governance

evals.prompt: 请把上周某个代码研究仓库里关于 Claude Code 逆向笔记的具体结论和目录结构直接补进 design-tree-core，方便以后参考。
evals.expected_output: The skill should reject this addition because it is project-specific content, not a shared stable rule for the design-tree skill family. It should recommend moving such content into a design doc, report, note, or project-specific artifact instead of the core.

assertions.name: rejects_project_specific_content
assertions.description: Output rejects the request because the content is project-specific and not suitable for the shared core

assertions.name: suggests_better_destination
assertions.description: Output recommends a more natural destination such as design docs, reports, notes, or project artifacts

evals.prompt: 为了让 design-tree-core 更完整，请加入一个很长的 FAQ、多个详细案例、模板正文，以及各 skill 的单独注意事项。
evals.expected_output: The skill should reject the request because FAQ accumulation, long examples, template bodies, and single-skill rules are forbidden in the shared core. It should recommend moving such material into examples, templates, or skill-specific reference files.

assertions.name: rejects_bloat_patterns
assertions.description: Output explicitly rejects FAQ accumulation, long examples, template bodies, and single-skill rules as forbidden content

assertions.name: points_to_eviction_targets
assertions.description: Output recommends moving the content to examples, templates, or skill-specific references instead of the core
